ads.title: Brand introduction image
ads.primary_text:
The Steelers' wealth management partner is hosting a complimentary evening at Westmoreland Country Club.

Tuesday, May 5 at 6:00 PM. Our CEO, President (CFP, CPA), CIO (CFA, CFP), and Director of Wealth Planning (CFA, CFP) sit down for an honest conversation about what's on your mind financially.

The economy. Estate planning. Charitable giving. The pitfalls nobody warns you about.

Cocktails and hors d'oeuvres included.

We won't follow up unless you ask us to. Seating is limited.
ads.headline: An Evening of Clarity
ads.hook: The Steelers' wealth management partner is hosting an evening for Pittsburgh families.

**Photo direction:** Use branded office photos from `assets/photos/branded/Highlights/` — leadership team across table from a couple or family, natural conversation posture, warm overhead lighting. Conference room or meeting setting. If no suitable group meeting photo is available, use stock-website advisor/client meeting images from `assets/photos/stock-website/ConfluenceWeb Images/`.

**Text overlay:** ~10% coverage. Headline in bottom third over gradient. Logo + Steelers badge small top-center.

**Gradient:** Bottom-up — transparent at 40%, dark navy 65% opacity at 100%.

ads.title: Educational carousel (5 cards)
ads.primary_text:
Four leaders. Six topics. One evening at Westmoreland Country Club. You're invited.

Tuesday, May 5 at 6:00 PM. Our CEO, President, CIO, and Director of Wealth Planning will cover six topics worth your attention.

Your financial future. Estate planning. Charitable giving. Common pitfalls. The economy. Technology in financial planning.

Complimentary cocktails and hors d'oeuvres. We won't follow up unless you ask us to. Seating is limited.
ads.headline: What Our Leadership Panel Covers
ads.hook: Four firm leaders. Six topics. One evening at Westmoreland Country Club.

cards.label: Hook
cards.caption:
Your financial plan touches investments, estate documents, taxes, family goals.

Most of the time, nobody's looking at all of them together. That's what this evening is about.

cards.label: Economy & Portfolio
cards.caption:
What does the current economy mean for your portfolio?

Our CIO, Bill Winkeler (CFA, CFP), will walk through what he's watching in the markets and what it means for the years ahead.

cards.label: Estate & Family Meetings
cards.caption:
When was the last time your family sat down to talk about the plan?

Estate documents get drafted and filed. Life changes. We'll discuss what a productive family meeting looks like.

cards.label: Charitable Giving & Pitfalls
cards.caption:
Charitable giving is one of the most misunderstood parts of a financial plan.

We'll also cover the common pitfalls we see families make.

cards.label: CTA
cards.caption:
Tuesday, May 5 at 6:00 PM
Westmoreland Country Club, Export, PA

Complimentary cocktails and hors d'oeuvres. Seating is limited. Reserve yours.

ads.title: Personal invite image
ads.primary_text:
The financial conversations worth having don't happen in conference rooms. Or on sales calls.

They happen over cocktails, at a country club, with the people who actually run the firm.

Tuesday, May 5. Our CEO, President (CFP, CPA), CIO (CFA, CFP), and Director of Wealth Planning (CFA, CFP) are hosting an evening at Westmoreland Country Club.

The economy. Estate planning. Charitable giving. The questions most people put off too long.

Complimentary. We won't follow up unless you ask us to. We'd be glad to meet you.

ads.hook: The financial conversations worth having don't happen in conference rooms.

**Photo direction:** Use the Greg Weimer headshot from `assets/photos/panelists/` — warm, approachable. Alternatively, a branded office photo of Greg in a professional setting. This ad humanizes the firm through its CEO. The photo should feel like a personal invitation, not a corporate portrait.

**Key difference from video version:** The static image puts the CEO's face front and center without the production overhead. The photo-under layout (headshot top, brand panel bottom) creates the same parasocial connection the video would have.

ads.title: Peace of mind image
ads.primary_text:
You're in the years where the decisions compound the most.

Portfolio. Estate. Tax strategy. What you do in the next few years shapes everything after.

Here's what most people don't ask until later: are all those pieces actually working together?

Tuesday, May 5. Our leadership team is hosting a complimentary evening at Westmoreland Country Club. Cocktails, a panel on six topics that hit close to home, and our CEO, President (CFP, CPA), CIO (CFA, CFP), and Director of Wealth Planning (CFA, CFP).

We won't follow up unless you ask us to. Limited seating.
ads.headline: Are the Pieces Working Together?
ads.hook: You're in the years where the decisions compound the most.
ads.image_direction:
**Format:** 1080x1080 (square) | **Placement:** Feed
**Layout:** Photo-dominant (Archetype 1) — matches inspiration Ads 01/02 most closely

```
COMPOSITION:
+--------------------------------------+
|  LOGO (top-center, navy)             |
|                                      |
|  [Lifestyle photo: couple (50s-60s)  |
|   in Pittsburgh-area setting —       |
|   autumn park, suburban garden,      |
|   or country club grounds.           |
|   Relaxed posture, warm tones,       |
|   open sky or foliage above.]        |
|                                      |
|  "Confidence in                      |
|   What You've Built."                |
|  (Ivypresto, dark navy, centered     |
|   — positioned in open sky area)     |
|                                      |
|  [RESERVE YOUR SEAT] (outlined)      |
|                                      |
+--------------------------------------+
```

**Photo direction:** Lifestyle couple in warm outdoor Pittsburgh-area setting. Key: natural light, relaxed body language, autumn foliage or green park landscape visible. Text overlays onto open sky area (no gradient needed if sky provides contrast). Logo in navy (not white) since background is light. Use `assets/photos/stock-website/ConfluenceWeb Images/` for couple lifestyle photos — select images that feel Pittsburgh-appropriate (no beach, no palm trees, no waterfront).

**This is the emotional anchor ad.** The photo should feel aspirational but real — "this is your life, we help you protect it." The setting should feel like Western Pennsylvania, not a resort.

**Photo direction:** Pittsburgh landmark photo — the city IS the subject. Pittsburgh skyline at golden hour from Mt. Washington, Point State Park fountain with bridges in background, or the confluence of the three rivers with fall foliage. One strong image, not a montage. The photo should make a Pittsburgh resident feel pride — "this is my city, this is my life."

**Steelers badge:** Small, centered below the headline — reinforces the local connection without dominating. The Steelers badge is the accent that says "this firm is part of this city."

**Key visual difference from other ads:** No faces, no advisor — this is about PLACE and IDENTITY. The viewer sees their own city reflected.

ads.title: Infographic / visual concept
ads.primary_text:
Having a financial plan isn't the same as having all the pieces working together.

With coordination: estate documents current, portfolio aligned, family conversations happening, charitable giving structured.

Without it: advisors in silos, estate documents gathering dust, market reactions instead of a strategy.

Tuesday, May 5. Our CEO, President, CIO, and Director of Wealth Planning are hosting a complimentary evening at Westmoreland Country Club.

Six panel topics. Cocktails and hors d'oeuvres. Seating is limited.
ads.headline: Coordinated vs. Fragmented
ads.hook: Having a plan isn't the same as having all the pieces working together.
ads.infographic_direction:
Left column, "Coordinated":
- Forward-looking strategy reviewed regularly
- Family meeting facilitation and communication planning
- Estate documents reviewed and coordinated with the full plan
- Charitable giving strategies integrated into the plan
- Portfolio strategy aligned with current conditions and long-term goals
- Technology connecting the pieces

Right column, "Fragmented":
- Reacting to markets without a forward view
- Family conversations put off or avoided
- Estate documents outdated or conflicting
- Charitable intentions unfulfilled
- Portfolio disconnected from the broader plan
- Advisors and documents in separate silos
ads.image_direction:
**Format:** 1080x1080 (square) | **Placement:** Feed
**Layout:** Split comparison (custom — not directly from archetypes)

```
COMPOSITION:
+--------------------------------------+
|  LOGO (top-center, white)            |
|                                      |
+------------------+-------------------+
|  COORDINATED     |  WITHOUT          |
|  (gold accent)   |  COORDINATION     |
|  (Rival Sans,    |  (white 60%)      |
|  label)          |  (Rival Sans)     |
|                  |                   |
|  [Dark navy      |  [Darker navy     |
|   #152d4b]       |   #0b1a2e]        |
|                  |                   |
|  ✓ Trusted       |  ✗ Court-appointed|
|    decision-     |    decision-      |
|    makers named  |    makers         |
|                  |                   |
|  ✓ Roth timed    |  ✗ Tax timing     |
|    with tax      |    works against  |
|    picture       |    you            |
|                  |                   |
|  ✓ Estate docs   |  ✗ Outdated or    |
|    up to date    |    conflicting    |
|                  |    documents      |
|  ✓ Heirs         |                   |
|    prepared      |  ✗ Family left    |
|                  |    guessing       |
|  ✓ Charitable    |                   |
|    giving        |  ✗ Intentions     |
|    structured    |    unfulfilled    |
|                  |                   |
+------------------+-------------------+
|                                      |
|  [RESERVE YOUR SEAT] (outlined)      |
|  Tue, May 5 | Westmoreland CC        |
+--------------------------------------+
```

**Design direction:** Two-column comparison on solid navy. Left column (coordinated) uses gold checkmarks (#c9a84c) and slightly lighter navy background. Right column (uncoordinated) uses white/red X marks and darker navy. Thin vertical divider line (white, 1px, 30% opacity) separates columns.

**Diagonal line texture:** Lines-02.png at 5% opacity across the full background — adds brand texture to what would otherwise be a flat navy card.

**Typography:** Ivypresto for the column headers ("Coordinated Plan" / "Without Coordination"). Rival Sans Light for the bullet points. All white text.

**This is the one ad that breaks the photo-dominant pattern.** The infographic comparison IS the visual. Keep it clean, readable at phone size. Max 5 items per column.

No video production needed — the headshot + credentials + insight headline work as a single compelling image.

**Layout:** Text-forward (Archetype 4) — urgency/scarcity

```
FEED (1080x1080):
+--------------------------------------+
|  [Solid dark navy #0b1a2e]           |
|                                      |
|  [Diagonal line pattern,             |
|   Lines-02.png, 10% opacity,        |
|   full background texture]           |
|                                      |
|  LOGO (top-center, white)            |
|                                      |
|                                      |
|  "A Few Seats Left."                 |

|   centered, bold)                    |
|                                      |
|  Tuesday, May 5                      |
|  Westmoreland Country Club           |

|                                      |
|  [Thin gold accent line]            |
|                                      |
|  [RESERVE YOUR SEAT]                 |
|  (outlined, gold border + text)      |
|                                      |
+--------------------------------------+

STORIES (1080x1920):
+--------------------+
|                    |
| [Solid dark navy   |
|  + diagonal lines  |
|  texture]          |
|                    |
| LOGO (white)       |
|                    |
|                    |
| "A Few Seats Left" |
| (Ivypresto, white, |
|  LARGE — 96px)     |
|                    |
| Tuesday, May 5     |
| (Rival Sans)       |
|                    |
| 4 C-Suite Leaders  |
| Cocktails & Panel  |
| (Rival Sans, 70%)  |
|                    |
| [RESERVE YOUR SEAT]|
+--------------------+
```

**Design direction:** This is the ONE text-forward ad in the campaign. No photo. The scarcity message IS the visual — "A Few Seats Left" dominates the frame. Dark navy background with the diagonal line texture adds brand identity without distracting. Gold accent color used for the divider line and CTA border — the only ad that uses gold prominently. This signals urgency within the brand system.

**Why no photo:** The number is the scroll-stopper. A photo would dilute the urgency. Inspiration precedent: PPC Ad 03 uses a similar text-heavy layout on navy background.

**Note:** Use "A Few Seats Left" rather than a specific number until client confirms venue capacity.

stories.image: Text-forward urgency design — stripped back, bold typography, minimal elements

reels.image: Same stripped-back design optimized for Reels format
reels.caption: Our evening at Westmoreland Country Club is this Tuesday, May 5. A few seats left. Cocktails, hors d'oeuvres, and all four of our firm's leaders: CEO, President, CIO, Director of Wealth Planning. Reserve below.
reels.description: A few seats left | This Tuesday | Westmoreland Country Club

ads.image_direction:
**Format:** 1080x1920 (vertical) | **Placement:** Stories, Reels
**Layout:** Text-forward urgency — designed but stripped back

```
STORIES/REELS:
+--------------------+
|                    |
| [Solid dark navy   |
|  background with   |
|  diagonal lines    |
|  at 8% opacity]    |
|                    |
| LOGO (white,       |
|  top-center)       |
|                    |
|                    |
| "This Tuesday."    |
| (Ivypresto, white, |
|  LARGE — 80px,     |
|  centered)         |
|                    |
| May 5 at 6:00 PM   |
| Westmoreland       |
| Country Club       |
| (Rival Sans,       |
|  white 70%)        |
|                    |
| [Thin gold line]   |
|                    |
| Cocktails. Panel.  |
| No obligation.     |
| (Rival Sans,       |
|  white 60%)        |
|                    |
| [RESERVE YOUR SEAT]|
| (outlined, white)  |
+--------------------+
```

**Design direction:** This is the LAST ad the audience sees. Unlike Ad 8 (which uses gold urgency), Ad 9 is stripped back — white on navy, minimal elements, no photo. The feeling should be quiet urgency, like a final personal note. "This Tuesday." as a standalone sentence is the entire hook.

**Key difference from Ad 8:** Ad 8 leads with scarcity ("A Few Seats Left" + gold accent). Ad 9 leads with proximity ("This Tuesday." + stripped design). Both are text-forward but feel different — Ad 8 is louder, Ad 9 is quieter. The progression from loud urgency to quiet reminder mirrors how you'd actually remind a friend.

**Why not raw/selfie video anymore:** The original plan called for a raw iPhone selfie video for authenticity. With the image-only pivot, this ad achieves the "last personal touch" through stripped-back design — fewer elements, more white space, quieter tone. It intentionally looks less produced than the rest of the campaign.
